original_prompt: Perform the following tasks in the order they are given: 1. What are we going to do on June 13 according to the file containing the vacation plans for Hawaii? 2. Please create a new file named 'hawaii-packing-list.docx' containing the packing list for the vacation plans in Hawaii based on the other document. Then, share the document with john.doe@gmail.com with read permissions
assessment_reasoning: The user request specifies two ordered tasks and the target filename and recipient, so the overall goal is clear. Minor ambiguities remain: the prompt refers to “the file containing the vacation plans for Hawaii” and “the other document” without giving an explicit filename or location, and it doesn’t specify the sharing mechanism or exact contents/formatting requirements for the packing list. These are small implementation details the agent can reasonably infer from the environment, so the prompt is slightly ambiguous but largely actionable.

variations.L1: I have some Hawaii trip files. Find out what we do on June 13 and make a packing list file from the trip info, then share it with John.
variations.L2: Open the file that contains the vacation plans for Hawaii. 1) Tell me what is scheduled for June 13 according to that file. 2) Create a new file named "hawaii-packing-list.docx" containing a packing list based on those vacation plans and share it with john.doe@gmail.com. (You can choose the permission level.)
variations.L3: Please do these two things in order: (1) From the document that contains the Hawaii vacation plans (the trip planning doc in my files), tell me exactly what we are going to do on June 13. (2) Create a new Word document named "hawaii-packing-list.docx" that contains a packing list derived from the Hawaii vacation plans document, and then share that new document with john.doe@gmail.com with read-only access. Return confirmation once shared.
variations.L4: Perform the following tasks in order: 1) What are we going to do on June 13 according to the file containing the vacation plans for Hawaii? 2) Create a new file named "hawaii-packing-list.docx" containing the packing list for the vacation plans in Hawaii based on the other document. Then share the document with john.doe@gmail.com with read permissions.
variations.L5: Do these steps exactly and report back: (A) Open the source document named "vacation-plans-hawaii.docx" in my files and extract the schedule item for June 13. Return the extracted text as plain text. (B) Create a new Microsoft Word file named "hawaii-packing-list.docx" (MIME type application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document). Populate it with a clearly labeled packing list derived from the source document: include sections for Clothing, Toiletries, Electronics, Documents, and Optional/Activity-specific items, and ensure quantities or counts where indicated in the source. Save the file to the same folder as the source. (C) Share that new file with john.doe@gmail.com by adding a permission with type=user and role=reader (read-only). After sharing, return the new file's path/ID and a one-line confirmation that the file was shared with john.doe@gmail.com as reader.
